---
title: "fara"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
#load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(dplyr)
library(DT)
library(lubridate)
library(tidyr)
library(leaflet)
```

```{r}
#load data sets
fara <- read.csv("data/fara_docs_fixed.csv") %>% 
  clean_names()
```

```{r}
#I am now going to make this data set only last from 2012-2024
fara$date <- as.Date(fara$date, format="%m/%d/%Y")
fara$year <- as.numeric(format(fara$date, "%Y"))

# Filter dates between 2012 and 2024
filtered_fara <- subset(fara, format(date, "%Y") >= 2012 & format(date, "%Y") <= 2024)

print(filtered_fara)
write.csv(filtered_fara, "~/Downloads/filtered_fara.csv",row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
grouped_fara <- filtered_fara %>% 
  group_by(year) %>% 
  count()
```

```{r}
# Sort the data by year and registration number
filtered_fara <- filtered_fara[order(filtered_fara$year, filtered_fara$registration_number),]

# Initialize a vector to keep track of seen registration numbers
seen_registrations <- c()

# Create an empty dataframe with the same structure as filtered_fara
filtered_fara_unique <- filtered_fara[0,]

# Loop through each row and keep only new registration numbers
for (i in 1:nrow(filtered_fara)) {
  reg_num <- filtered_fara$registration_number[i]
  if (!(reg_num %in% seen_registrations)) {
    seen_registrations <- c(seen_registrations, reg_num)
    filtered_fara_unique <- rbind(filtered_fara_unique, filtered_fara[i, ])
  }
}

# View the final filtered dataframe
print(filtered_fara_unique)
```

```{r}
group_unique <- filtered_fara_unique %>% 
  group_by(year) %>% 
  count()
```

```{r}
write.csv(group_unique, "~/Downloads/group_unique.csv",row.names = FALSE)
```
